data.description:
Classify every LLM failure into the closed resolution-disposition enum paired with a verbatim diagnostic, replacing the opaque `except Exception -> LLMUnavailableError` collapse.

## Why
`src/rebar/llm/runner.py:349-361` flattens auth, 429, 529, timeout, and content-filter into one `LLMUnavailableError` — no caller can tell "retry" from "buy quota" from "fix config" from "the agent is looping." Downstream gates, messaging, and telemetry need a typed disposition plus the raw truth for troubleshooting.

## What
Introduce `classify_llm_failure(exc, ctx) -> LLMOutcome{resolution_class, diagnostic, retryable}` called at the single runner except-seam. Map exception -> one of the 8 closed classes; carry a verbatim `diagnostic` (exception type, status_code, provider body error-code, finish_reason, Retry-After, attempts, trace_id, model, provider). Split `LLMUnavailableError` into typed subclasses carrying the outcome (back-compat: they subclass `LLMUnavailableError`, so existing `except` sites still catch). Catch `httpx.*` directly (confirmed: they propagate unwrapped). Discriminate `UnexpectedModelBehavior` subclasses (`ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`) rather than bucketing the base class. Total + defensive: an unmatched failure -> NEEDS_INVESTIGATION with full diagnostic; the classifier itself never raises. `UsageLimitExceeded` defaults to FIX_AGENT_DESIGN (not CHANGE_SETTINGS), with the diagnostic carrying step/tool-call counts to disambiguate loop-vs-legit.

## Scope
- The closed 8-class enum + the `LLMOutcome` value object + the typed error hierarchy.
- Provider-body inspection for the terminal-vs-transient 429 split (e.g. OpenAI `insufficient_quota`) — best-effort, behind the reliable binary retry-or-not core.
- Only the binary retryable decision is behaviour-critical; the class is a best-effort hint (a misread wastes <= N bounded retries, never a safety issue).
- NOT in scope: the retry mechanism (story: retry transport) or the gate wiring (story: gate posture).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `classify_llm_failure` maps every RED-suite failure class to the correct `resolution_class` + `diagnostic`; unknown -> NEEDS_INVESTIGATION.
- [ ] Catches `httpx.*` directly; discriminates `UnexpectedModelBehavior` subclasses (`ContentFilterError`, `IncompleteToolCall`).
- [ ] Typed errors subclass `LLMUnavailableError` (existing catch sites still work).
- [ ] The classifier is total and never raises (property test over arbitrary exceptions).
- [ ] The corresponding RED-suite cases flip GREEN; `make test`/`make lint`/`make typecheck` green.

Files: `src/rebar/llm/runner.py`, `src/rebar/llm/errors.py`, a new classifier module under `src/rebar/llm/`.
data.title: Classify LLM failures into the resolution-disposition enum + verbatim diagnostic
